# Output Header Format

Both generators produce files matching the project's existing convention in
`dv/simulation/verif_env/soc/header_files/c_header` and `dv/simulation/verif_env/soc/header_files/sv_header`.

## C Header (`<IP>RegDef.h`)

```c
#ifndef __<IP>_REG_DEF_H__
#define __<IP>_REG_DEF_H__

/* ============================================================================
 * <IP> Register Definition (C / BSP Header)
 * ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 * Auto-generated by reg-def-gen skill from <source-label>
 * Layout: APB/AHB/AXI byte-addressable, 32-bit data width.
 * ...
 * Base address: 0xXXXXXXXX.
 * ============================================================================
 */

/* ===== Section N ===== */

/* REG_NAME (offset) - description */
typedef union
{
    struct
    {
        unsigned int field /* field, R/W, description */ : W;
        unsigned int /* rsvd_HI_LO */ : W;     // gap-fill or explicit reserved
        ...
    }bits;
    unsigned int u32;
} U_<IP>_<REG_NAME>;

/* Address Base & Per-Register OFFSET / ADDR Macros */

#define <IP>_REG_BASE_ADDR                          0xXXXXXXXX
#define <IP>_<REG_NAME>_REG_OFFSET                  0xNNN
#define <IP>_<REG_NAME>_REG_ADDR                    (<IP>_REG_BASE_ADDR + <IP>_<REG_NAME>_REG_OFFSET)
...

#endif //__<IP>_REG_DEF_H__
```

### Field-emission rules (C)

| Source JSON            | Emitted struct line                                                                  |
|------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `{reserved: true}`     | `unsigned int /* rsvd_HI_LO */ : W;`                                                |
| `{reserved: false}`    | `unsigned int <name> /* <name>, <access>, <first-line of desc> */ : W;`              |
| Variable-width (x:N)   | Same as above with trailing `, variable width (x:N), default=W bits` in the comment |
| Gap-fill (undocumented)| `unsigned int /* rsvd_HI_LO */ : W; /* Reserved Field: Yes (gap-fill) */`            |

The field width `W` is the resolved (or fixed) width.  The struct field
declarations are emitted in **MSB-first** order so the C source reads top-down
matching the databook tables.

### Macro rules (C)

| Macro                                 | Purpose                                           |
|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
| `<IP>_REG_BASE_ADDR`                  | One global macro, hex literal                     |
| `<IP>_<REG>_REG_OFFSET`               | One per register, taken from `meta.offset`        |
| `<IP>_<REG>_REG_ADDR`                 | One per register: `BASE_ADDR + _REG_OFFSET`       |

## SV Header (`<IP>RegDef.svh`)

```systemverilog
/* <IP> Register Definition (SystemVerilog Header) */
#ifndef __<IP>_REG_DEF_SVH__
#define __<IP>_REG_DEF_SVH__

/* ... file header (same shape as C, "C / BSP Header" replaced by "SystemVerilog") */

/* ===== Section N ===== */

/* REG_NAME (offset) - description */
typedef union {
    struct packed {
        bit[W-1:0]        field /* field, R/W, comment */;
        const bit[W-1:0]  /* rsvd_HI_LO */;
        ...
    }bits;
    int unsigned u32 = {W'h0, W'h0, ...};     // one entry per struct field
} U_<IP>_<REG_NAME>;

`define <IP>_REG_BASE_ADDR                      'hXXXXXXXX
`define <IP>_<REG_NAME>_REG_OFFSET              0xNNN
`define <IP>_<REG_NAME>_REG_ADDR                (`<IP>_REG_BASE_ADDR + `<IP>_<REG_NAME>_REG_OFFSET)
...

#endif //__<IP>_REG_DEF_SVH__
```

### Field-emission rules (SV)

| Source JSON            | Emitted struct line                                                  |
|------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `{reserved: true}`     | `const bit` or `const bit[W-1:0]` (1-bit slots use single-bit form)  |
| `{reserved: false}`    | `bit` or `bit[W-1:0]`                                                |
| Variable-width (x:N)   | The resolved width is used; the SV variable marker `x:N` is irrelevant once resolved |
| Gap-fill               | `const bit` / `const bit[W-1:0]` with `/* rsvd_HI_LO */`             |

The struct fields are emitted in **MSB-first** order, matching databook reading
order.  The `int unsigned u32 = {...}` initializer concatenates a `'h0` literal
for every struct field, MSB-first, so the initializer widths also sum to 32.

### Macro rules (SV)

Same shape as C, but:

- Each macro is `` `define `` (back-tick form).
- `_REG_ADDR` expands with `` ` `` prefixes around its operands.

## Why gaps are auto-filled

Synopsys databooks often list a register's named/typed fields and leave
**undocumented bits** (sometimes reserved, sometimes simply unmentioned).
Without gap-fill, the resulting struct would sum to less than 32 and the
validator would complain.  Gap-fill is a deliberate, automatic fill so the
generated headers are bit-faithful without needing to hand-edit each one.

If you do **not** want gap-fill (e.g. you want to keep parity with the
databook verbatim), pass `--no-gap-fill` to either generator (the parser and
the example already include this toggle).  Currently gap-fill is always on;
to turn it off, edit the `fill_field_gaps` function in both generators.
